title: juniperus communis subsp. depressa
intro: Juniperus communis subsp. depressa, also known as the low-growing juniper, is a subspecies of the juniper tree, characterized by its compact, spreading habit and needle-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and terpenes. The health benefits of juniperus communis subsp. depressa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal uses.
